settings.title: Settings
settings.tabs.api-config.name: API Configuration
settings.tabs.features.name: Features
settings.tabs.browser.name: Browser
settings.tabs.terminal.name: Terminal
terminal.outputLimitDescription: Maximum number of lines to include in terminal output when executing commands. When exceeded, lines will be removed from the middle, saving tokens.
terminal.subagentOutputLimitDescription: Maximum number of lines to include in output from CLI subagents. Truncates middle to save tokens.
settings.tabs.general.name: General
settings.tabs.about.name: About
settings.tabs.debug.name: Debug
general.telemetry.lockedByOrg: This setting is managed by your organization's remote configuration
general.telemetry.allow: Allow sending usage and crash reports
general.telemetry.description.pre: Help improve ShunCode by sending usage data and crash reports. Code, prompts, or personal information are never sent. See our
general.modelLanguageDescription: Controls which language the AI model uses in its responses. Independent from the interface language above.
chat.contextHint: Use @ for context, / for commands, Shift + drag for files/images
chat.modePlanDescription: gather information to prepare a plan
chat.modeDebugDescription: systematically debug issues step by step
chat.modeChatDescription: answer questions without proactively exploring the project
apiConfig.separateModels: Use separate models for Planning and Action modes
apiConfig.separateModelsDescription: Switching between Planning and Action modes preserves the API and model used in the previous mode. This can be useful, for example, when using a stronger reasoning model for planning and a cheaper coding model for execution.
about.description: An AI assistant that can use your CLI and Editor. ShunCode can solve complex software tasks step by step using tools that let it create and edit files, explore large codebases, use the browser, and execute terminal commands (with your approval).
terminal.defaultProfileDescription: Choose the default terminal profile used by ShunCode. 'Default' uses your global VS Code settings.
terminal.timeoutDescription: Set how long ShunCode waits for shell integration before running commands. Increase it if terminal connection timeouts occur.
terminal.reuseDescription: When enabled, ShunCode reuses existing terminal windows outside the current working directory. Disable this if tasks get stuck after terminal commands.
terminal.executionModeDescription: Choose whether ShunCode runs commands in the VS Code terminal or in a background process.
chat.mistakeLimitReached: The model made several consecutive errors
chat.createsPatchesForFile: ShunCode is creating patches to edit a file:
chat.wantsToEditFile: ShunCode wants to edit this file:
chat.fileOutsideWorkspace: This file is outside the workspace
chat.wantsToDeleteFile: ShunCode wants to delete this file:
chat.wantsToCreateFile: ShunCode wants to create a new file:
chat.wantsToReadFile: ShunCode wants to read this file:
chat.wantsToListTopLevelFiles: ShunCode wants to list top-level files in folder:
chat.listedTopLevelFiles: ShunCode listed top-level files in folder:
chat.wantsToListFilesRecursive: ShunCode wants to recursively list all files in folder:
chat.listedFilesRecursive: ShunCode recursively listed all files in folder:
chat.wantsToListCodeDefinitions: ShunCode wants to list code definition names in this folder:
chat.listedCodeDefinitions: ShunCode listed code definition names in this folder:
chat.wantsToSearchInFolder: ShunCode wants to search in folder
chat.wantsToFetchUrl: ShunCode wants to fetch URL content:
chat.wantsToWebSearch: ShunCode wants to search the web:
chat.shellIntegrationWarningText: ShunCode may not read command output correctly. Please update VS Code
chat.shellIntegrationWarningMiddle: and make sure you use a supported shell: zsh, bash, fish, or PowerShell
chat.shellIntegrationIssuesDescription: Because of frequent shell integration issues, we recommend switching to Background Terminal for better reliability.
chat.wantsToStartNewTask: ShunCode wants to start a new task:
chat.wantsToCondenseConversation: ShunCode wants to condense the conversation:
chat.wantsToCreateGithubIssue: ShunCode wants to create a GitHub issue:
features.subagents.cliRequired: Subagents require ShunCode CLI. Install it:
features.subagents.authHint: to authenticate in ShunCode or configure an API provider.
features.subagents.description: Allows ShunCode to run subprocesses for focused tasks, such as exploring large codebases, while keeping the main context clean.
features.enableCheckpointsDescription: Allows the extension to save workspace checkpoints during task execution. Uses git, which can perform poorly on very large workspaces.
features.mcpDisplayModeDescription: Controls how MCP responses are shown: plain text, formatted with links/images, or markdown rendering.
features.openaiReasoningEffortDescriptio: Reasoning effort level for OpenAI model families (applies to all OpenAI model providers).
features.strictPlanModeDescription: Forces tool usage restrictions in plan mode, preventing file edits.
features.focusChainDescription: Enables improved task progress tracking and automatic focus chain list management across tasks.
features.focusChainIntervalDescription: Interval (in messages) for reminding ShunCode about its focus chain checklist (1-100). Lower values produce more frequent reminders.
focusChain.newSteps: New steps will be generated if you continue the task
focusChain.clickToEdit: Click to edit to-do list in file
features.autoCondenseDescription: Enables an advanced context management system that uses LLM-based condensation for next-generation models.
features.shuncodeWebToolsDescription: Enables local web search (DuckDuckGo) and web page content fetching (Chromium) tools. No authentication required.
features.shuncodeWebToolsWarning: ⚠️ Enabling this will download the Chromium browser (~120 MB). Please wait for the download to complete before using.
features.shuncodeWebToolsAuthRequired: Authentication required to use web tools.
features.worktreesDescription: Enables git worktree management for running ShunCode tasks in parallel.
features.nativeToolCallDescription: Uses the model's native tool-calling API instead of XML-based tool parsing. This improves performance for supported models.
features.parallelToolCallingDescription: Allows models to call multiple tools in a single response. Automatically enabled for GPT-5 models.
features.backgroundEditDescription: Allows editing files in the background without opening the editor diff view.
features.simplifiedEditToolsDescription: Uses delete_block and replace_text instead of replace_in_file. Recommended for weaker models (<70B, free tiers).
features.validateSyntaxBeforeApplyDescri: Uses Tree-sitter to validate code syntax before applying changes.
features.blockOnSyntaxErrorsDescription: Rejects changes if they introduce new syntax errors and reports this back to the model.
features.multiRootDescription: Allows ShunCode to work with multiple workspaces.
features.skillsDescription: Enables Skills for reusable agent instructions loaded from .shuncode/skills/ directories.
features.lockedByOrg: This setting is managed by your organization's remote configuration
features.yoloDanger: EXPERIMENTAL AND DANGEROUS: This mode disables safety checks and user confirmations. ShunCode will auto-approve all actions without asking. Use with extreme caution.
browser.disableToolUseDescription: Prevent ShunCode from using browser actions (launch, click, type, etc.).
browser.viewportDescription: Set browser viewport size for screenshots and interactions.
browser.remoteDescriptionPrefix: Enable using your Chrome in ShunCode
browser.remoteDescriptionSuffix: . You can specify a custom path below. Using remote browser connection requires running Chrome in debug mode
browser.remoteHostHint: or by using the button below. Enter host address or leave empty for auto-discovery.
browser.customArgsDescription: Space-separated arguments passed to browser executable.
debug.syntaxDescription: Tree-sitter validates code before applying changes.
debug.diffDescription: Manage View Zones and pending changes to debug Cursor-like diffs.
debug.resetStateDescription: This resets all global state and secret storage in the extension.
provider.noteText: ShunCode uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not perform as expected.
provider.lightweightModeDescription: Reduces the prompt size and number of tools for less capable models. Resets automatically when you change the model.
litellm.reasoningDescription: Enhanced reasoning is available for models like Sonnet-4, o3-mini, Deepseek R1, etc. Learn more about
litellm.unifiedDescription: LiteLLM provides a unified interface to access models from different LLM providers. See their
preferredLanguage.label: Preferred language
preferredLanguage.description: Language ShunCode uses to communicate with you.
preferredLanguage.alwaysThinkDescription: Forces the model to use the selected language for internal reasoning (Chain of Thought). This may improve reasoning quality in your native language, but can reduce overall performance for some models.
apiOptions.providerLockedByOrg: Provider settings are managed by your organization's remote configuration
taskHeader.clickToScrollToYourMessage: Click to scroll to your message
taskHeader.clickContextWindowToSetThresh: Click on the context window bar to set a new threshold.
taskHeader.thresholdAutoCondenseDescript: When the context window usage exceeds this threshold, the task will be automatically condensed.
taskHeader.noKeepTaskAsIs: No, keep the task as is
taskHeader.currentTokensUsedInRequest: Current tokens used in this request
taskHeader.maxContextWindowForModel: Maximum context window size for this model
chat.retryRevertAndResend: Retry: revert changes and resend this message
chat.deleteRevertFromMessage: Delete: revert all changes from this message onwards
chat.deleteDialog.title: Delete Message
chat.deleteDialog.description: Would you like to delete only the chat messages, or also revert all file changes made from this point?
chat.revertDialog.title: Confirm revert
chat.copyFormattedHint: Copy with formatting for Word, Google Docs, and email
chat.copyMarkdownHint: Copy as Markdown source (for editors, chats, and .md files)
chat.shuncodeWantsToUseSubagent: ShunCode wants to use a subagent:
chat.commandRunningInVscodeTerminal: This command is running in the VSCode terminal. You can manually stop it using Ctrl+C in the terminal, or switch to Background Execution mode in settings for cancellable commands.
chat.commandRequiresApproval: The model has determined this command requires explicit approval.
chat.windowsPowershellIssue: It seems like you're having Windows PowerShell issues, please see this
chat.searchPatternNoMatchRetrying: The model used search patterns that don't match anything in the file. Retrying...
chat.enableExtendedThinking: Enable extended thinking for enhanced reasoning
chat.useDifferentModelsPlanAct: Use different models for Plan vs Act
chat.hookTimeoutHint: Took longer than 30 seconds. Check for infinite loops or add timeouts to network requests.
chat.hookInvalidJsonHint: Hook returned invalid JSON. See error details below for more information.
chat.autoApproveDescription: Let ShunCode take these actions without asking for approval.
chat.startingRecording: Preparing voice input... First launch may take a few minutes
chat.viewManageWorktreesHint: View and manage git worktrees. Great for running parallel ShunCode tasks.
chat.outOfCredits: You have run out of credits.
chat.shuncodeWantsToUseBrowser: ShunCode wants to use the browser:
checkpoint.restoreTaskAndWorkspaceDesc: Restores the task and your project's files back to a snapshot taken at this point
checkpoint.restoreTaskOnlyDesc: Deletes messages after this point (does not affect workspace)
checkpoint.restoreWorkspaceOnlyDesc: Restores your project's files to a snapshot taken at this point (task may become out of sync)
telemetry.collectsUsageData: ShunCode collects error and usage data to help us fix bugs and improve the extension. No code, prompts, or personal information is ever sent.
telemetry.turnOffIn: You can turn this setting off in
banner.sidebarTip.title: Quick Start
banner.sidebarTip.description: To get started, sign up at [OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai) — one key gives access to 200+ models, including free ones. [Learn more](https://shuncode-ai.ru/docs/models)
checkpoint.label: Checkpoint
checkpoint.restoreFilesAndTaskDesc: Revert files and clear messages after this point
checkpoint.error.slowInit: Checkpoints are taking longer than expected to initialize. Working in a large repository? Consider re-opening ShunCode in a project that uses git, or disabling checkpoints.
checkpoint.error.timeout: Checkpoints initialization timed out. Consider re-opening ShunCode in a project that uses git, or disabling checkpoints.
checkpoint.error.gitRequired: Git must be installed to use checkpoints.
dialog.unsavedChangesDescription: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?
history.title: History
shuncodeRules.rulesDescription: Rules allow you to provide ShunCode with system-level guidance. Think of them as a persistent way to include context and preferences for your projects or globally for every conversation.
shuncodeRules.workflowsDescription: Workflows allow you to define a series of steps to guide ShunCode through a repetitive set of tasks, such as deploying a service or submitting a PR. To invoke a workflow, type
shuncodeRules.skillsDescriptionPrefix: Skills are reusable instruction sets that ShunCode can activate on-demand. When a task matches a skill's description, ShunCode uses the
shuncodeRules.skillsDescriptionSuffix: tool to load the full instructions.
shuncodeRules.hooksDescription: Hooks allow you to execute custom scripts at specific points in ShunCode's execution lifecycle, enabling automation and integration with external tools.
shuncodeRules.hookTogglingWindowsBanner: Hook toggling is not supported on Windows. Hooks can be created, edited, and deleted, but cannot be enabled/disabled and will not execute.
shuncodeRules.agentsTooltip: Searches recursively for all AGENTS.md files in the workspace when a top-level AGENTS.md exists
shuncodeRules.ruleRequiredCannotDisable: This rule is required and cannot be disabled
shuncodeRules.hookTogglingNotSupportedWi: Hook toggling not supported on Windows. Hooks can be edited and deleted, but won't execute.
shuncodeRules.skillNameValidation: Skill name can only contain letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores
shuncodeRules.allowedExtensions: Only .md, .txt, or no file extension allowed
mcp.addRemoteServerDescription: Add a remote MCP server by providing a name and its URL endpoint. Learn more
mcp.configureDescriptionMiddle: enables communication with locally running MCP servers that provide additional tools and resources to extend ShunCode's capabilities. You can use
mcp.configureDescriptionSuffix: or ask ShunCode to create new tools specific to your workflow (e.g., "add a tool that gets the latest npm docs").
mcp.orgManagesSomeServers: Your organization manages some MCP servers
mcp.serverCannotBeDisabledByOrg: This server can't be disabled because it is enabled by your organization
mcp.name: Name
mcp.orgPreconfiguredAvailableServers: Your organization has pre-configured the available MCP servers
mcp.noServersInMarketplace: No MCP servers found in the marketplace
mcp.communityMadeUseAtYourOwnRisk: Community Made (use at your own risk)
provider.keyStoredLocally: This key is stored locally and only used to make API requests from this extension.
provider.optionalOllamaApiKeyHelp: Optional API key for authenticated Ollama instances or cloud services. Leave empty for local installations.
provider.ollamaUnableFetchModels: Unable to fetch models from Ollama server. Please ensure Ollama is running and accessible, or enter the model ID manually above.
provider.timeoutDescription: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for API responses before timing out.
provider.ollamaDescriptionPrefix: Ollama allows you to run models locally on your computer. For instructions on how to get started, see their
provider.routingDefaultDescription: Load balance across providers (AWS, Google Vertex, etc.), prioritizing price while considering uptime
provider.routingPriceDescription: Sort by price, prioritizing the lowest cost provider
provider.routingThroughputDescription: Sort by throughput, prioritizing highest throughput (may increase cost)
provider.routingLatencyDescription: Sort by response time, prioritizing lowest latency
provider.settingManagedByOrg: This setting is managed by your organization's remote configuration
provider.awsProfileCredentialsDescriptio: Using AWS Profile credentials from ~/.aws/credentials. Leave profile name empty to use the default profile. These credentials are only used locally to make API requests from this extension.
provider.awsCredentialsDescription: Authenticate by either providing the keys above or use the default AWS credential providers, i.e. ~/.aws/credentials or environment variables. These credentials are only used locally to make API requests from this extension.
provider.bedrockCustomHint: Select "Custom" when using the Application Inference Profile in Bedrock. Enter the Application Inference Profile ARN in the Model ID field.
provider.vertexAiSetupPrefix: To use Google Cloud Vertex AI, you need to
provider.failedToFetchModelsCheckConfig: Failed to fetch models. Please check your configuration.
provider.clientIdSetReenter: Client Id is set. To change it, please re-enter the value.
provider.clientSecretSetReenter: Client Secret is set. To change it, please re-enter the value.
provider.credentialsStoredLocallyOnly: These credentials are stored locally and only used to make API requests from this extension.
provider.sapAiCoreApiAccessInfo: You can find more information about SAP AI Core API access here.
provider.orchestrationEnabledDesc: When enabled, provides access to all available models without requiring individual deployments.
provider.orchestrationDisabledDesc: When disabled, provides access only to deployed models in your AI Core service instance.
provider.unableFetchSapModels: Unable to fetch models from SAP AI Core service instance. Please check your SAP AI Core configuration or ensure your deployments are deployed and running in the service instance
provider.configureSapCredentialsToSeeMod: Please configure your SAP AI Core credentials to see available models.
provider.getVercelApiKeyBy: You can get a Vercel AI Gateway API key by
provider.useModelsFromCopilot: Use models from your GitHub Copilot subscription. Install the
provider.enableClaudeInCopilot: enable Claude models in Copilot settings to get started.
provider.qwenApiLineDescription: Please select the appropriate API interface based on your location. If you are in China, choose the China API interface. Otherwise, choose the International API interface.
provider.qwenOauthCredentialsPathDescrip: Path to your Qwen OAuth credentials file. Use ~/.qwen/oauth_creds.json or provide a custom path.
provider.qwenCodeOauthDescription: Qwen Code is an OAuth-based API that requires authentication through the official Qwen client. You'll need to set up OAuth credentials first.
provider.oauthCredentialsStoredAutomatic: OAuth credentials will be stored automatically
provider.huggingFaceKeyDescription: This key is stored locally and only used to make API requests from this extension. We don’t show pricing here because it depends on your Hugging Face provider settings and isn’t consistently available via their API
provider.difyDescription: Dify is a platform that provides access to various AI models through a unified API. Configure your Dify instance URL and API key to get started.
provider.difyModelSelectionHandled: The model selection is handled within your Dify application configuration.
provider.pathToClaudeCodeCli: Path to the Claude Code CLI.
provider.notEditableReturnedByEndpoint: Not editable - the value is returned by the connected endpoint
provider.signInWithOracleCodeAssist: Sign in with Oracle Code Assist
provider.ocaSetupDescriptionPrefix: Please ask your IT administrator to set up Oracle Code Assist as a model provider. Oracle Employees, please see the
provider.failedRefreshModels: Failed to refresh models. Check your session or network.
provider.ideaForOracleCodeAssist: Have an idea for Oracle Code Assist?
provider.noteComplexPrompts: (Note: ShunCode uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not work as expected.)
provider.lmStudioDescriptionPrefix: LM Studio allows you to run models locally on your computer. For instructions on how to get started, see their
provider.lmStudioNeedStartPrefix: You will also need to start LM Studio's
provider.lmStudioNeedStartSuffix: feature with lms server start to use it with this extension.
account.welcomeDescription: Sign up for an account to get access to the latest models, billing dashboard to view usage and credits, and more upcoming features.
account.byContinuingAgree: By continuing, you agree to the
account.cannotChangeWhenRemoteConfigEnab: This cannot be changed while your organization has remote configuration enabled.
account.sayThanksSubtitle: ShunCode is free — but we'd love your support!
account.freeTrialWelcome: Welcome! 🎉 ShunCode is a completely free project. Try it right now — {{limit}} messages are available without signing up. To use without limits, just create a free account.
worktrees.title: Worktrees
worktrees.description: Git worktrees let you work on multiple branches at the same time, each in its own folder. Open worktrees in their own windows so ShunCode can work on multiple tasks in parallel.
worktrees.failedToCreateTask: Failed to create task for ShunCode
worktrees.deleteWorktreeDescription: This will delete the worktree directory at
worktrees.unpushedCommitsWarning: Warning: Unpushed commits on this branch will be lost.
worktrees.createDescriptionOpen: This will create a copy of your project on a new branch and open in a separate window.
worktrees.createDescription: This will create a copy of your project on a new branch.
worktrees.branchHelp: Your new copy will be checked out to this branch.
worktrees.folderHelp: Where the project will be copied for the worktree.
worktrees.createIncludeMiddle: file to automatically copy files like
worktrees.createIncludeSuffix: to new worktrees, so you don't have to reinstall dependencies.
onboarding.changeLaterInSettings: You can change this later in settings
welcome.intro:
👋 欢迎使用顺码！

🌱 顺码是一款免费、开源、可高度自定义的 AI 编程助手。

🔓 模型、提示词、工具和记忆都由你掌控。你可以接入自己的模型，修改系统提示词，扩展工具链，并管理 AI 记住的内容。

🧩 不是黑盒 Agent，而是由你掌控的开放 Agent。

🚀 Agent 的上限，由你自己决定。
welcome.description: I can help you with all kinds of coding tasks — create and edit files, explore projects, use the browser, and run terminal commands (with your permission). I also support MCP for extending my capabilities.
welcome.apiKeyHint: To get started, sign in or configure an API key for access to AI models.

settings.tabs.voice.name: Voice
voice.enableDescription: Dictate your requests by voice. No internet required. Maximum 5 minutes per recording.
voice.languageDescription: The language you will be dictating in.
voice.modelDescription: Higher quality means more accurate recognition and punctuation, but slower processing.
voice.setupRequired: Click to set up voice input
voice.authRequired: Please sign in to your ShunCode account to download components.
voice.downloadNote: First use or quality change requires a one-time download. This may take a couple of minutes.
voice.downloadRequired: Voice input components need to be downloaded.
voice.downloadNewModel: Components for selected quality need to be downloaded.
voice.microphoneDescription: Select a microphone for recording. "Auto" lets the system pick a working device.
settings.tabs.multiProvider.name: Multi Provider

systemPrompt.enableCustomDescription: Replace the default system prompt with a custom template. Use the variables below to include dynamic context.
systemPrompt.builtinReadonly: The built-in profile is read-only. Create or duplicate a profile to customize.
systemPrompt.templateDescription: Use {{VARIABLE_NAME}} to include dynamic context. Available variables are listed below.
systemPrompt.previewDescription: Preview of the generated system prompt with current context.
systemPrompt.previewPlaceholder: Click 'Refresh preview' to generate a preview...
systemPrompt.variablesDescription: Use these variables in your template. They will be replaced with actual values at runtime.

tools.description: Customize which tools ShunCode can use and how they are described to the model.
tools.sectionDescription: Enable or disable tools, and override tool and parameter descriptions used in prompts.
tools.clickRefresh: Click Refresh to load available tools.
memory.description: Memories are stored as global rule files and included in ShunCode context when enabled.
permissions.description: Control which actions ShunCode can perform without asking for approval.
